title: My Spirit Will Not Haunt The Mound
content:
My spirit will not haunt the mound
        Above my breast,
    But travel, memory-possessed,
    To where my tremulous being found
        Life largest, best.

    My phantom-footed shape will go
        When nightfall grays
    Hither and thither along the ways
    I and another used to know
        In backward days.

    And there you'll find me, if a jot
        You still should care
    For me, and for my curious air;
    If otherwise, then I shall not,
        For you, be there.
meaning:
Thomas Hardy's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "My Spirit Will Not Haunt The Mound"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
